metadata.title: GlobalProtect Certificate Best Practices
metadata.description:
The GlobalProtect components require valid SSL/TLS certificates to establish
        connections. The best practices include using a well-known, third-party CA for the portal
        server certificate, using a CA certificate to generate gateway certificates, optionally
        using client certificates for mutual authentication, and using machine certificates for
        pre-logon access.
metadata.keywords: 10.1 & Later, English, GlobalProtect, 10.1 & Later, GlobalProtect Administration, GlobalProtect Administration, Non-Cloud, PANW Yellow Theme, Administration, GlobalProtect Admin Guide

metadata.title: Deploy Server Certificates to the GlobalProtect Components
metadata.description:
Best practices for deploying server certificates to the GlobalProtect components
                include importing certificates from a well-known CA, creating a root CA certificate
                for self-signed certificates, using SCEP for certificate requests, and assigning
                certificates to SSL/TLS service profiles.
metadata.keywords: 10.1 & Later, English, GlobalProtect, 10.1 & Later, GlobalProtect Administration, GlobalProtect Administration, Non-Cloud, PANW Yellow Theme, Administration, GlobalProtect Admin Guide

        type. Deployment methods include SCEP and local firewall certificates.

        profile to authenticate the user and follow a workflow to create and deploy the client

metadata.title: Deploy User-Specific Client Certificates for Authentication
metadata.description:
To enable individual user authentication with GlobalProtect, issue and deploy unique
        client certificates to endpoints. You can automate this by configuring the GlobalProtect
        portal as a Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) client to a SCEP server in the
        enterprise PKI. Two-factor authentication can also be set up using the SCEP profile.

        secure access for mobile workforce.

metadata.title: SSO Wrapping for Third-Party Credential Providers on Windows Endpoints
metadata.description:
GlobalProtect app on Windows allows SSO by wrapping native credential provider, but
        may fail with third-party providers. Configuring wrapping resolves the issue.
metadata.keywords: 10.1 & Later, Tabs, English, GlobalProtect, 10.1 & Later, GlobalProtect Administration, GlobalProtect Administration, Non-Cloud, PANW Yellow Theme, Network Security, Administration

Ensure Endpoint Compliance with HIP Checks

metadata.title: Ensure Endpoint Compliance with HIP Checks
metadata.description:
The GlobalProtect app collects host information and submits it to the GlobalProtect

metadata.title: Information Collected
metadata.description:
Learn how the MDM integration service translates information collected from endpoints
        managed by Workspace ONE into HIP report attributes.

metadata.title: Addressed Issues
metadata.description:
See the list of addressed issues in GlobalProtect app 6.0 for Android, iOS, Chrome,
        Windows, Windows 10 UWP, macOS, and Linux.
metadata.keywords: English, GlobalProtect Release Notes, 6.0, GlobalProtect Release Notes, GlobalProtect, Release Notes, Non-Cloud, PANW Yellow Theme, 6.0

text:
GlobalProtect Overview
Next
What OS Versions are Supported with GlobalProtect?

metadata.title: GlobalProtect Overview
metadata.description:
GlobalProtect solves the security challenges introduced by roaming users by extending
        next-generation firewall-based policies to all users, no matter where they are located.
metadata.keywords: 10.1 & Later, English, GlobalProtect, 10.1 & Later, Getting Started, GlobalProtect Getting Started Guide, Non-Cloud, PANW Yellow Theme

Next

What OS Versions are Supported with GlobalProtect?

text:
About GlobalProtect Certificate Deployment
Previous
Enable SSL Between GlobalProtect Components

metadata.title: About GlobalProtect Certificate Deployment
metadata.description:
There are three approaches to deploying server certificates to GlobalProtect
        components: a combination of third-party and self-signed certificates, using an enterprise
        Certificate Authority (CA), or using self-signed certificates.

metadata.title: GlobalProtect Certificate Best Practices
metadata.description:
The GlobalProtect components require valid SSL/TLS certificates to establish
        connections. The best practices include using a well-known, third-party CA for the portal
        server certificate, using a CA certificate to generate gateway certificates, optionally
        using client certificates for mutual authentication, and using machine certificates for
        pre-logon access.
metadata.keywords: 10.1 & Later, English, GlobalProtect, 10.1 & Later, GlobalProtect Administration, GlobalProtect Administration, Non-Cloud, PANW Yellow Theme, Administration, GlobalProtect Admin Guide

        authentication beginning with GlobalProtect app 5.0.

metadata.title: Deploy User-Specific Client Certificates for Authentication
metadata.description:
To enable individual user authentication with GlobalProtect, issue and deploy unique
        client certificates to endpoints. You can automate this by configuring the GlobalProtect
        portal as a Simple Certificate Enrollment Protocol (SCEP) client to a SCEP server in the
        enterprise PKI. Two-factor authentication can also be set up using the SCEP profile.
